message:
fix(hooks): properly clean up particle canvas on unmount and rewrite Konami detection

use-click-particles.ts:
- Extract cleanupGlobalResources() helper that tears down the shared canvas,
  cancels the animation frame, clears the particle array, and removes the
  window resize listener in one atomic operation
- Call cleanupGlobalResources() when the last active hook instance unmounts,
  preventing orphaned canvas elements and resize listeners in SPAs
- Track the resize handler in a module-level globalResizeHandler variable and
  guard against registering duplicate listeners on repeated getCanvas() calls
- Use Math.max(0, ...) when decrementing _activeHookCount to prevent underflow
- Capture globalCanvas into a local variable at the start of animate() and
  validate canvas identity inside the rAF loop, so a stale closure never draws
  to a removed canvas

use-konami.ts:
- Switch key matching from event.code (physical key position, e.g. "KeyB") to
  event.key (logical character), normalizing single-character keys to lowercase
  so the sequence works regardless of keyboard layout
- Replace the sliding-window array approach with a single sequenceIndexRef
  counter that advances on match and resets on mismatch, reducing per-keystroke
  allocations from O(n) array operations to O(1) integer comparisons
- Add isEditableTarget() guard to skip detection when focus is inside inputs,
  textareas, selects, or contenteditable elements
- Ignore key events with modifier keys (ctrl, meta, alt, shift), repeat flag,
  or IME composing state to avoid false progress through the sequence
- Define KONAMI_CODE as a const tuple and derive the KonamiKey type from it
  for compile-time exhaustiveness

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
